inlineContent.text: Audio and haptic feedback is an important part of the typing experience, where a keyboard can trigger audio and haptic feedback when a user taps on a key or performs certain actions.
In KeyboardKit, feedback can be triggered with a
developer guide has step-by-step instructions on how to trigger and customize audio & haptic feedback for a keyboard.
namespace that contains feedback-related types, like
enums that define supported feedback types,
with observable state and auto-persisted feedback
, that can be used to customize the audio and haptic feedback for various gestures and actions.
. You can use the context to extensively customize the audio and haptic feedback.
The feedback customization capabilities will move to the
class in KeyboardKit 11. This transition will start later in the KeyboardKit 10 lifecycle, to prepare you for this change.
KeyboardKit automatically creates an instance of this class and registers it with its
, and also injects it into the view environment.
types that can be used to define more complex feedback behavior.
can be used to trigger audio & haptic feedback as users interacts with the keyboard. The
protocol also implements this protocol, and will trigger feedback as actions are triggered.
. You can replace it to customize how the keyboard triggers feedback.
type with auto-persisted settings that can be used to customize the feedback behavior.
is used by default. KeyboardKit Pro unlocks a
that can be used to handle these settings.
that can be used to manage audio and haptic feedback settings:

abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit feedback engine.
Register custom haptic feedback.
This screen can be used as a settings screen for any
essential keyboard settings.
Represents feedback for an error event.
This namespace contains keyboard feedback-related types.
The audio configuration to use when enabled.
Represents the sound of an input key.
This article describes the KeyboardKit external keyboard engine.
Toggle haptic feedback enabled state.
This article describes the KeyboardKit callout engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit navigation engine.
Keyboard-specific services.
This article describes the KeyboardKit dictation engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit autocomplete engine.
This enum defines standard audio feedback types.
This developer guide describes how to customize keyboard feedback.
This struct can be used to configure audio feedback.
Represents the sound of a delete key.
This engine can be used to trigger audio feedback.
Whether haptic feedback is enabled, by default
This article describes the KeyboardKit localization engine.
Register custom audio feedback.
This standard service can be used to trigger various
audio and haptic feedback, using system capabilities.
This enum defines standard haptic feedback types.
Represents feedback for a successful event.
This article describes the KeyboardKit AI support capabilities.
This type has auto-persisted feedback settings.
Represents feedback for a warning event.
This article describes the KeyboardKit input engine.
This article describes the KeyboardKit emoji engine.
This article describes how KeyboardKit Pro lets you define and use a host application.
This article describes KeyboardKit’s clipboard support.
This screen can be used as a settings screen for any
feedback-related keyboard settings.
Feedback-specific, auto-persisted settings.
This protocol can be implemented by any classes that can
handle triggered
events.
This article describes KeyboardKit’s custom font support.
This class has observable states and persistent settings
for keyboard feedback.
This article describes the KeyboardKit app-specific utilities.
This article links to the various KeyboardKit features.
This article describes the KeyboardKit action engine.
Toggle audio feedback enabled state.
Whether audio feedback is enabled, by default
Keyboard-specific state.
This article describes the KeyboardKit layout engine.
This struct can be used to configure haptic feedback.
The haptic configuration to use when enabled.
This protocol can be implemented by any type that can be
used to trigger audio and haptic feedback.
Represents the sound of a system key.
This engine can be used to trigger haptic feedback.
This screen can be used as the main home screen of a
keyboard app that should look like iOS Settings.

KeyboardKit helps you create fully customizable keyboard extensions, using Swift and SwiftUI.
This enum defines keyboard-specific actions, and is also
a namespace for action-related types.
